content.text:
Agora tenho uma visão clara. Deixe-me consolidar tudo numa análise holística completa:

---

## ANÁLISE HOLÍSTICA COMPLETA DO PROJETO MMS

### 1. CONTEXTO DO NEGÓCIO

**Situação Crítica:**
- Cliente: **Keolis MHI** (operador do Dubai Metro)
- Valor: **$147,500** (total todas as fases)
- Status: **EM RISCO** - SoW confusa gerou dúvidas no cliente
- Pergunta que revelou tudo: *"Have you done this before?"*

**O que o cliente precisa:**
1. **Prova** de que funciona (não promessas)
2. Materiais para justificar internamente (ROI, case studies)
3. Confiança de que a escolha não vai prejudicar a carreira de quem aprova

**Fases Comerciais:**
| Fase | Valor | Escopo |
|------|-------|--------|
| Phase 0 | $12,500 | Demo/Playground (ATUAL) |
| Phase 1 | $55,000 | MVP completo |
| Phase 2 | $35,000 | Módulo de custos |
| Phase 3 | $45,000 | Integração Maximo |

---

### 2. ARQUITETURA ATUAL DO SISTEMA

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        FRONTEND (React Router)                   │
│                    apps/mms/ (Cloudscape Design)                │
│                                                                  │
│  Routes: dashboard, fleet, schedule, what-if, calendar          │
│  Lib: demo-data.ts (gera dados sintéticos), api.ts (fetch)      │
└─────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘
                              │ HTTP POST /api/solver/solve
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        API (Hono + Bun)                          │
│                      packages/api/                               │
│                                                                  │
│  - Validação Zod                                                │
│  - Rate limiting                                                │
│  - camelCase ↔ snake_case conversion                            │
└─────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘
                              │ subprocess (stdin JSON → stdout)
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     SOLVER (Python + HiGHS)                      │
│                      packages/solver/                            │
│                                                                  │
│  - MIP optimization                                             │
│  - 16/16 testes passando                                        │
│  - <3 segundos para 128 trens × 52 semanas                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

      ❌ NÃO HÁ BANCO DE DADOS CONECTADO NO FLUXO ATUAL
```

---

### 3. O QUE EXISTE vs O QUE FALTA

| Componente | Status | Detalhes |
|------------|--------|----------|
| **Python Solver** | ✅ FUNCIONA | 16/16 testes, HiGHS MIP, <3s |
| **API Server** | ✅ FUNCIONA | Hono + Bun, validação, rate limit |
| **What-If Simulator** | ✅ FUNCIONA | Página principal da demo |
| **Demo Data Generator** | ✅ FUNCIONA | 79 Alstom + 49 Kinki corretos |
| **Dashboard** | ⚠️ PARCIAL | Existe mas usa dados estáticos |
| **Fleet Page** | ⚠️ PARCIAL | Existe mas dados não persistem |
| **Calendar View** | ❓ A VERIFICAR | Arquivo existe |
| **Excel Export** | ❌ NÃO EXISTE | Necessário para demo |
| **Database** | ❌ NÃO CONECTADO | CRÍTICO - necessário para demo |
| **Mileage Tracking Real** | ❌ NÃO EXISTE | Solver não rastreia km real |

---

### 4. ANÁLISE DO SOLVER vs REQUISITOS RFQ

**O que o RFQ pede:**

| Requisito RFQ | Status no Solver | Gap |
|---------------|------------------|-----|
| "Fetching daily mileage for each train" | ❌ | Não há integração com dados reais |
| "Predicting maintenance schedules based on **real-time mileage**" | ⚠️ PARCIAL | Usa mileage sintética, não real |
| "Adjust schedule based on available resources" | ✅ | Man-hours constraint funciona |
| "Short-term and long-term plans (weekly, monthly, yearly 1-10)" | ⚠️ | Só tested até 52 semanas |
| "Warnings on mileage underrun/overrun" | ❌ | Não implementado (sistema RUC) |
| "Simulations on various planning options" | ✅ | What-If Simulator funciona |
| "Recommend train numbers based on fitness/mileage" | ❌ | Run Assignment não existe |
| "Recommend run assignment for each train" | ❌ | Não implementado |
| "Interface between maintenance and operations teams" | ❌ | Não existe |

**Validação da Técnica MIP:**

Baseado na pesquisa web REAL:
- [Wiley Research](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/mice.12764): MIP falha em 62% dos casos grandes (>600 jobs, >8000 opções)
- [Hong Kong MTR](https://www.cs.cityu.edu.hk/~hwchun/AIProjects/stories/allocation/mtrcscheduling/): Usa **Genetic Algorithm + Heuristics**, não MIP puro
- [Deutsche Bahn](https://pubsonline.informs.org/doi/10.1287/inte.2020.1069): Usa **Hypergraph optimization** (não MIP simples)

**Conclusão:** MIP + HiGHS é válido para o **problema simplificado** atual. Para o sistema completo de produção, pode precisar de híbrido GA/LNS + MIP.

---

### 5. UI LIBRARY: Cloudscape vs Elastic UI

**Situação Atual:**
- Usando **Cloudscape Design** (AWS) - `@cloudscape-design/components`
- Você pediu para usar **Elastic UI** (`@elastic/eui`)

**Comparação:**

| Aspecto | Cloudscape (atual) | Elastic UI |
|---------|-------------------|------------|
| Origem | AWS | Elastic (Kibana) |
| Look | Enterprise/AWS | Data-focused |
| Charts | Incluso | Incluso (EuiCharts) |
| Tables | Excelente | Excelente |
| Licença | Apache 2.0 | Dual (Elastic v2/SSPL) |
| Migração | - | ~2-3 dias de trabalho |

**Minha Avaliação:**
- Cloudscape já está funcionando
- Trocar para Elastic UI agora = **risco para demo**
- Recomendo: manter Cloudscape para demo, avaliar Elastic UI pós-contrato

---

### 6. DATABASE: MongoDB vs PostgreSQL

**Situação Atual:**
- `packages/db/` existe com código sofisticado (document-client, mongo-loader)
- MAS: não está conectado ao fluxo da aplicação
- Frontend usa `demo-data.ts` gerando dados em memória

**Para a Demo, você precisa:**
1. Dados persistentes (não in-memory)
2. Demonstrar que o sistema pode carregar/salvar

**Opções:**
| Opção | Prós | Contras |
|-------|------|---------|
| MongoDB | Código já existe em `packages/db` | Precisa setup (Atlas ou local) |
| PostgreSQL | Mais familiar, SQL | Precisaria refatorar db package |
| SQLite | Zero setup, arquivo local | Limitado para produção |
| JSON file | Trivial para demo | Não escala |

**Minha Recomendação:** 
Para demo → SQLite ou JSON persistido
Para produção → MongoDB (código já existe)

---

### 7. O QUE A DEMO PRECISA DEMONSTRAR

Segundo `DEMO-STRATEGY.md`:

**MUST HAVE (P0):**
1. ✅ Rodar solver com 128 trens em <3s
2. ✅ Mostrar before/after workload
3. ⚠️ Calendar View (arquivo existe, verificar se funciona)
4. ❌ Excel Export
5. ❌ Database persistente

**SHOULD HAVE (P1):**
- PDF Report
- Dashboard com métricas reais
- ROI Calculator display

**NICE TO HAVE (P2):**
- Case Studies page
- Maximo CSV preview

---

### 8. BLOCKERS PARA DEMO FUNCIONAL

| Blocker | Severidade | Esforço Estimado |
|---------|------------|------------------|
| Database não conectado | **CRÍTICO** | 1-2 dias |
| Excel Export não existe | **ALTO** | 0.5-1 dia |
| Calendar View não verificado | **MÉDIO** | Verificar se funciona |
| Dados não persistem entre sessões | **CRÍTICO** | Dependente de DB |

---

### 9. FLUXO DE DADOS COMPLETO (COMO DEVERIA SER)

```
┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐
│   MongoDB    │────▶│   API Server │────▶│   Frontend   │
│   (assets,   │     │   (Hono)     │     │   (React)    │
│   schedules) │◀────│              │◀────│              │
└──────────────┘     └──────┬───────┘     └──────────────┘
                           │
                           ▼
                    ┌──────────────┐
                    │Python Solver │
                    │   (HiGHS)    │
                    └──────────────┘
```

**Atualmente:** Frontend → API → Solver funciona, mas dados não persistem em DB.

---

### 10. RESUMO EXECUTIVO

**O que FUNCIONA:**
- ✅ Solver Python (MIP/HiGHS) - 16/16 testes
- ✅ API Server (Hono/Bun)
- ✅ What-If Simulator (core da demo)
- ✅ Dados sintéticos realistas (79 Alstom + 49 Kinki)

**O que está PARCIAL:**
- ⚠️ Dashboard, Fleet, Schedule pages (existem mas sem dados reais)
- ⚠️ Calendar page (existe, não verificado)

**O que está FALTANDO:**
- ❌ Database conectado (BLOCKER)
- ❌ Excel Export (necessário para demo)
- ❌ PDF Report
- ❌ Mileage tracking em tempo real
- ❌ Run Assignment

**Risco Técnico da Abordagem MIP:**
- Para demo: ✅ OK
- Para produção longo prazo: ⚠️ Pode precisar de híbrido (GA + MIP)

---

**Perguntas para você:**

3. Qual é a data da demo?

Based on my search, here's what I found about Elastic UI (EUI) components, charts, tables, calendar, data grid, and features:

## Elastic UI Framework Overview

The Elastic UI Framework (EUI) is a design library in use at Elastic to build React applications that need to share their branding and aesthetics. It distributes typed UI React components and static assets for use in building web layouts.(no content)Alongside the React components, they ship theme & style utilities that can be used independently on their own. The primary goal of this library is to provide reusable UI components that can be used throughout Elastic's web products.(no content)EUI offers a wide variety of components. The documentation is organized topically with individual pages containing live examples, usage recommendations, guidelines, and props.

## Data Grid

The EUI data grid allows you to optionally define an in-memory level to have the grid automatically handle updating your columns. Depending upon the level chosen, you may need to manage the content order separately from the grid. Schemas allow you to tailor the render and sort methods for each column.(no content)The EUI tables are great, but they don't provide the level of density and scalability needed for tools like Discover, data viz and ML. This led to the creation of a data grid component to deal with those needs.(no content)This component is primarily aimed at data analysis needs.

## Tables

EUI provides both opinionated and non-opinionated ways to build tables.(no content)EuiBasicTable handles mobile row selection, row actions, row expansion, and mobile UX out of the box with relatively simple-to-use APIs. It is best used with asynchronous data, or static datasets that do not need pagination/sorting.(no content)EuiInMemoryTable has all the features that EuiBasicTable has, and additionally handles pagination, sorting, and searching the passed data out-of-the-box with relatively minimal APIs. It is best used with smaller synchronous datasets.(no content)If your table requires completely custom behavior, you can use individual building block components like EuiTable, EuiTableRow, EuiTableRowCell, and more.

## Charts

The Metric chart is a single value visualization available from Elastic Charts. It allows you to represent a metric, a KPI, a specific state with a very simple interface, similar to EuiStat but with more data visualization oriented capabilities.(no content)The value can be associated with a secondary visual representation that takes place in the background (like a progress bar or a trend), providing a contextual information layer.

## Layout Features

EUI provides flex groups, grids, panels and items to build responsive page layout, as well as components with strictly defined height to organize content blocks.(no content)Elastic UI provides a set of helpful page templates which offer an easy way to create different page layout patterns. You can also build the page yourself by stringing together various components.

For more details, you can check out the official documentation at https://eui.elastic.co/ and the GitHub repository at https://github.com/elastic/eui.

Links: [{"title":"Data visualization - Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://cloudscape.design/patterns/general/data-vis/"},{"title":"Charts - Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://cloudscape.design/components/charts/"},{"title":"Components overview - Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://cloudscape.design/components/"},{"title":"What is AWS Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://www.hava.io/blog/what-is-aws-cloudscape-design-system"},{"title":"Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://cloudscape.design/"},{"title":"AWS open sources Cloudscape Design System • DEVCLASS","url":"https://devclass.com/2022/07/21/aws-open-sources-cloudscape-design-system/"},{"title":"GitHub - cloudscape-design/components: React components for Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://github.com/cloudscape-design/components"},{"title":"Announcing Cloudscape Design System, an open source solution for building intuitive web applications - AWS","url":"https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2022/07/cloudscape-design-system-open-source-solution-building-intuitive-web-applications/"},{"title":"Cloudcraft – Draw AWS diagrams","url":"https://www.cloudcraft.co/"},{"title":"Charts (Legacy) - Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://cloudscape.design/components/charts-legacy/"}]

Here's what I found about Cloudscape Design System's components, charts, tables, and data visualization features:

## Overview

Cloudscape is an open source design system to create web applications. It was built for and is used by Amazon Web Services (AWS) products and services.(no content)AWS created it in 2016 to improve the user experience across web applications owned by AWS services, and also to help teams implement those applications faster.(no content)Cloudscape offers a solid base of 60+ components, 30+ pattern guidelines, and 20+ demos to make your work easier.

## Chart Components

Charts are a graphic representation of information and quantitative data, built on the Highcharts library, designed to provide a clear and interactive way to convey meaning.

### Available Chart Types

These include cartesian charts which makes up line, bar, mixed, scatter, area, and pie and donut charts. Specifically:

- **Line chart**: Visualizes one or many series of data, with an emphasis on how the data changes over time.
- **Bar chart**: Visualizes one or many series of data, with an emphasis on the total amount of each data point.
- **Mixed chart**: Visualizes different, but related, series of data on a single chart.
- **Scatter chart**: Visualizes the relationships between data in two dimensions.
- **Area chart**: Visualizes two or more series of data. Through stacked data series, it emphasizes the part-to-whole relationship of data over a period of time.
- **Pie and donut charts**: Display portions of a whole unit so users can compare data points from a total set.(no content)These new chart components require appropriate Highcharts licenses. Cloudscape is not responsible for improper usage of these Highcharts-based components or lack of license coverage.

## Data Visualization Objectives

The objective of data visualization is for users to be able to quickly and clearly derive meaning from a set of data. Data visualization supports four common objectives: Identifying trends - users want to understand how a metric or set of metrics is changing over time.(no content)**Identify aberrations/anomalies**: Users want to spot deviations from a normal or expected range, often the significant increase or decrease of a particular metric.(no content)**Comparison**: Users want to identify commonalities or divergences between two or more metrics.(no content)**Investigate problems**: Users have been notified of a problem or found one during a review or investigation, and use a visualization to better understand what happened.

## Color Palettes for Data Visualization

The Status and Severity Palette uses colors to indicate the level of severity of the observed data. The colors range from red through to orange, yellow and green to show the severity level and also uses blue and grey to indicate a neutral status. A Generic Categorical Palette has 50 colors used in combination to provide contrast in charts.(no content)The Threshold Color Palette contains colors to help define thresholds on line and bar charts and use the colors to reinforce the significance of the threshold.

## Additional Features

Components are built with React, they are tested, accessible, and responsive.(no content)To customize your user experience, Cloudscape offers theming, dark mode, and content density modes.(no content)It offers a scalable and flexible foundation that meets high standards for accessibility, styles, and internationalization.(no content)All Cloudscape packages are available on npm. You can install them using `npm install @cloudscape-design/components @cloudscape-design/global-styles`.

Links: [{"title":"Calendar - Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://cloudscape.design/components/calendar/"},{"title":"Date picker - Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://cloudscape.design/components/date-picker/"},{"title":"Date range picker - Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://cloudscape.design/components/date-range-picker/"},{"title":"Date Picker Component in Design Systems","url":"https://designsystems.surf/components/date-picker"},{"title":"[Feature Request]: Time Picker · Issue #2421 · cloudscape-design/components","url":"https://github.com/cloudscape-design/components/issues/2421"},{"title":"Components overview - Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://cloudscape.design/components/"},{"title":"How change display date format of data picker / range? · cloudscape-design/components · Discussion #1417","url":"https://github.com/cloudscape-design/components/discussions/1417"},{"title":"Time input - Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://cloudscape.design/components/time-input/"},{"title":"Date picker – Carbon Design System","url":"https://v10.carbondesignsystem.com/components/date-picker/usage/"},{"title":"Date picker","url":"https://carbondesignsystem.com/components/date-picker/usage/"}]

Here are the search results for "Cloudscape Design calendar component date picker scheduling":

## Cloudscape Design System - Date & Calendar Components

The Cloudscape Design System offers several components for date and time selection:

### Calendar Component
With the calendar component, users can select dates. This is a core component available in the Cloudscape Design System.

### Date Picker
With the date picker, users can enter or choose a date value. This provides a user-friendly interface for date selection.

### Date Range Picker
With the date range picker, users can specify a date and time range. This is useful when users need to select a period of time rather than a single date.

### Time Input
A form element in which a user can enter an absolute time value.

### Component Details
Components are built with React and implement the design patterns and guidelines of Cloudscape. All components are tested, responsive, and accessible.

### Date Format Limitations
The default display date format is yyyy/mm/dd.(no content)The components currently don't support changing the display date format and they don't have short-term plans to implement this. They did take a note for the request and will use it as a data point when planning future date-related components enhancements.

### Feature Requests
There has been community interest in additional time functionality. The existing time input component lacks intuitiveness and ease of use; integrating a time picker would significantly enhance the user experience.(no content)Users have noted that what is missing is the simplicity to simply click the calendar button and select the date & time together. The way it is now, the calendar button allows selection of the date only, then another box needs to be added that allows the user to type in the time. The user then needs to enter values into 2 boxes to get the date/time.

You can find the official documentation at:
- Calendar: https://cloudscape.design/components/calendar/
- Date Picker: https://cloudscape.design/components/date-picker/
- Date Range Picker: https://cloudscape.design/components/date-range-picker/
- Time Input: https://cloudscape.design/components/time-input/

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Elastic UI (EUI) calendar date picker and scheduling components:

## EUI Date Picker Components

### EuiDatePicker
EuiDatePicker is the foundational component that all other EUI date and time controls are built upon. It depends upon moment for all of its formatting, and a forked and modified version of react-datepicker for accessibility.(no content)At minimum, the EuiDatePicker requires the `selected` and `onChange` props to be passed.

Key features include:
- Two props control time selection: `showTimeSelect` will make time selection appear next to the calendar and `showTimeSelectOnly` will exclude the calendar and make the time selection the only thing you see.
- You can use the `inline` prop to display the date picker directly in the page instead of inside a popover.
- You can use `customInput` to pass a custom input to trigger your calendar.
- You can use `utcOffset` to apply an offset to the datetime.
- Locale formatting is achieved by using the `locale`, `timeFormat`, and `dateFormat` props. The latter will take any moment() notation.

### EuiDatePickerRange
To create a single date range control, use EuiDatePickerRange and pass individual EuiDatePicker components into the `startDateControl` and `endDateControl` props. You can control the state of both inputs as direct props on EuiDatePickerRange as well as control each individually.

### EuiSuperDatePicker
EuiSuperDatePicker is a complex date picker that supports relative and absolute dates. It offers a convenient Quick select menu which includes Commonly used dates, Recently used date ranges and Auto refresh features.(no content)Pass start and end date times as strings in either datemath format (e.g., `now`, `now-15m`, `now-15m/m`) or as absolute date in the format `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.SSSZ`.

Quick select menu customization options:
- `commonlyUsedRanges`: A default set of common date ranges is automatically provided but can be overridden with this prop.
- `recentlyUsedRanges`: Store the users previously submitted time ranges and pass them back to date picker with this prop. It's best to limit this list to around 10 items.
- `customQuickSelectPanels`: Accepts an array of panel objects as `{ title: string, content: ReactElement }`.(no content)By supplying a callback function to `onRefreshChange`, the EuiSuperDatePicker will display the refreshInterval configuration UI in the Quick select menu.

### NPM Package
The Elastic UI Framework is a collection of React UI components for quickly building user interfaces at Elastic. Check out their full documentation site, which contains many examples of components in the EUI framework aesthetic, and how to use them in your products.

**Documentation Links:**
- Date Picker: https://eui.elastic.co/docs/components/forms/date-and-time/date-picker/
- Date Picker Range: https://eui.elastic.co/docs/components/forms/date-and-time/date-picker-range/
- Super Date Picker: https://eui.elastic.co/pr_8411/new-docs/docs/forms/date-picker/super/


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - aws-samples/cloudscape-examples: Examples of ready-to-use Cloudscape apps","url":"https://github.com/aws-samples/cloudscape-examples"},{"title":"Meet the New Elastic Cloud Enterprise UI! | Elastic Blog","url":"https://www.elastic.co/blog/meet-the-new-ece-ui"},{"title":"GitHub - elastic/eui: Elastic UI Framework 🙌","url":"https://github.com/elastic/eui"},{"title":"Cloudscape Design System","url":"https://cloudscape.design/"},{"title":"Elastic UI – Component library for data-driven web apps | Hacker News","url":"https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=33945219"},{"title":"AWS open sources Cloudscape Design System • DEVCLASS","url":"https://devclass.com/2022/07/21/aws-open-sources-cloudscape-design-system/"},{"title":"Cloudscape Demos - Service dashboard","url":"https://cloudscape.design/examples/react/dashboard.html"},{"title":"Cloudscape - AWS Design System - Made with React.js","url":"https://madewithreactjs.com/cloudscape"},{"title":"Elastic UI Framework | Elastic UI Framework","url":"https://eui.elastic.co/"},{"title":"Elastic UI Alternatives and Similar Sites & Apps | AlternativeTo","url":"https://alternativeto.net/software/elastic-ui/"}]

Based on the search results, here's a comparison of **Cloudscape** vs **Elastic UI (EUI)** for React enterprise dashboards:

## Cloudscape Design System

Cloudscape is an open source design system to create web applications. It was built for and is used by Amazon Web Services (AWS) products and services. It was created in 2016 to improve the user experience across web applications owned by AWS services, and also to help teams implement those applications faster.

**Key Features:**
- Built with React, the components are tested, accessible, and responsive.
- Currently includes 66 components, 36 pattern guidelines, and 28 demos. Designers also get a Figma design library, and the programming language is TypeScript.
- The components are firmly business-oriented, with charts – area, line, pie, bar and mixed charts – grids, date and date range pickers, and plenty of form components.
- The system is fantastic for designing consoles and dashboards to manage and monitor cloud resources.
- The system has several modes, light and dark for the general appearance, and comfortable versus compact for changing the density of the information displayed.

**Considerations:**
- The Cloudscape components have a distinctive look and feel developers will recognize as being AWS, though some work on theming could perhaps make that less obvious.

---

## Elastic UI (EUI)

The Elastic UI Framework (EUI) is a design library in use at Elastic to build React applications that need to share their branding and aesthetics. It distributes typed UI React components and static assets for use in building web layouts. Alongside the React components, they ship theme & style utilities that can be used independently on their own.

**Key Features:**
- The primary goal of this library is to provide reusable UI components that can be used throughout Elastic's web products. As React components, they remove CSS from the process of building UIs. As a single source of truth, the framework allows designers to make changes to aesthetics directly in the code, and unit test coverage for the UI components allows them to deliver a stable "API for user interfaces".
- The Elastic UI Framework began as a folder of code in Kibana and was decided it could be used beyond that codebase. It exists as an independent library so that patterns can be shared across teams and design standards can be scaled across the organization.
- The Elastic UI Framework is a collection of React UI components for quickly building user interfaces at Elastic, and you can still use the CSS behind each component even without React.
- Traditionally releases are made weekly against whatever is in the main branch.

**Licensing:**
- Dual-licensed under Elastic v2 and Server Side Public License, v1.

---

## Summary

| Aspect | Cloudscape | Elastic UI |
|--------|------------|------------|
| **Origin** | AWS (2016) | Elastic/Kibana |
| **Primary Use** | AWS console-style dashboards | Data-driven web apps |
| **Components** | 66+ components | Large component library |
| **Framework** | React + TypeScript | React (CSS also usable standalone) |
| **Design Tools** | Figma library included | - |
| **Theming** | Light/Dark, Comfortable/Compact modes | Theme & style utilities |
| **Best For** | Cloud management, AWS-style interfaces | Data visualization, Kibana-style apps |

Both are excellent choices for enterprise dashboards, with Cloudscape being more AWS-centric and EUI being more focused on data-driven applications in the Elastic ecosystem.

content.text:
---

## RELATÓRIO COMPLETO DO PROJETO MMS

### 1. COMPARAÇÃO UI: CLOUDSCAPE vs ELASTIC UI

| Feature | Cloudscape (atual) | Elastic UI |
|---------|-------------------|------------|
| **Charts (Bar, Line, Area)** | ✅ Incluso (Highcharts) | ✅ Incluso (Elastic Charts) |
| **Data Tables** | ✅ Excelente | ✅ Excelente + DataGrid |
| **Calendar/Date Picker** | ✅ Calendar, DatePicker, DateRangePicker | ✅ DatePicker, SuperDatePicker |
| **Forms** | ✅ Completo | ✅ Completo |
| **Modals** | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Progress/Status** | ✅ ProgressBar, StatusIndicator | ✅ Progress, Health |
| **Layout/Grid** | ✅ Grid, SpaceBetween | ✅ FlexGroup, Grid |
| **Export** | ❌ Manual | ❌ Manual |
| **Dark Mode** | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Licença** | Apache 2.0 | **Dual (Elastic v2/SSPL)** ⚠️ |
| **React 19** | ✅ Suportado | ❓ Verificar |

**MINHA RECOMENDAÇÃO:** 

🟢 **MANTER CLOUDSCAPE** para a demo. Razões:
1. Já está funcionando e implementado
2. Calendar View já existe e funciona (403 linhas)
3. What-If Simulator já funciona com charts
4. Trocar agora = **2-3 dias de retrabalho** = RISCO para demo esta semana
5. Licença mais simples (Apache 2.0)

Elastic UI é excelente, mas trocar agora é muito arriscado dado o prazo.

---

### 2. O QUE JÁ EXISTE E FUNCIONA

| Componente | Status | Linhas | Notas |
|------------|--------|--------|-------|
| **Python Solver** | ✅ FUNCIONA | ~300 | 16/16 testes, <3s |
| **API Server** | ✅ FUNCIONA | ~200 | Hono + Bun |
| **What-If Simulator** | ✅ FUNCIONA | 337 | Core da demo |
| **Calendar View** | ✅ FUNCIONA | 403 | Já implementado! |
| **Demo Data Generator** | ✅ FUNCIONA | 257 | 79 Alstom + 49 Kinki |
| **Dashboard** | ⚠️ Parcial | - | Precisa conectar a dados reais |
| **Fleet Page** | ⚠️ Parcial | - | Precisa conectar a dados reais |
| **MongoDB Client** | ✅ Código existe | ~1000+ | Em `packages/db/` |

**DESCOBERTA IMPORTANTE:** O Calendar View **JÁ EXISTE** e está funcional! Inclui:
- Navegação mensal
- Filtro por tipo de manutenção
- Visualização semanal com workload
- Modal de detalhes por semana
- Botões de Export (Excel/PDF/Maximo) - mas sem implementação ainda
- Summary stats (Total Events, Man-Hours, Avg Utilization, Peak Week)

---

### 3. BLOCKERS REAIS PARA DEMO

| Blocker | Severidade | O que falta | Esforço |
|---------|------------|-------------|---------|
| **Excel Export** | 🔴 ALTO | Botão existe mas não funciona | 4-6h |
| **Database Connection** | 🔴 CRÍTICO | MongoDB existe em packages/db mas não conectado | 1-2 dias |
| **Dados Persistentes** | 🔴 CRÍTICO | Schedules não salvam entre sessões | Depende de DB |
| **API conectada ao DB** | 🟡 MÉDIO | API não lê/escreve no MongoDB | 4-8h |

---

### 4. O QUE PRECISA SER FEITO PARA DEMO

**OPÇÃO A: Demo Mínima Viável (MVP) - Sem DB Persistente**

Se não der tempo de conectar MongoDB:
1. ✅ What-If Simulator já funciona
2. ✅ Calendar View já funciona
3. ❌ Implementar Excel Export (~4-6h)
4. Dados são gerados em memória (demo-data.ts)

**Risco:** Cliente pode perguntar "onde os dados são salvos?"

**OPÇÃO B: Demo Completa - Com MongoDB**

1. Configurar MongoDB Atlas (1h)
2. Conectar packages/db ao fluxo (4-8h)
3. API endpoints para CRUD de schedules (4h)
4. Frontend busca dados do DB (2-4h)
5. Implementar Excel Export (4-6h)

**Total:** ~2 dias de trabalho focado

---

### 5. ARQUITETURA COM MONGODB

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        FRONTEND (React Router)                   │
│                    apps/mms/ (Cloudscape)                       │
└─────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
        ┌─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┐
        │                     │                     │
        ▼                     ▼                     ▼
   GET /api/assets      POST /api/solver/solve   GET /api/schedules
        │                     │                     │
        └─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┘
                              │
┌─────────────────────────────▼───────────────────────────────────┐
│                        API (Hono + Bun)                          │
│                      packages/api/                               │
└────────────┬────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘
             │                │
             ▼                ▼
      ┌──────────┐    ┌──────────────┐
      │ MongoDB  │    │Python Solver │
      │ (Atlas)  │    │   (HiGHS)    │
      └──────────┘    └──────────────┘
```

---

### 6. RESUMO EXECUTIVO

**O que está BOM:**
- ✅ Solver funciona e é validado (MIP/HiGHS)
- ✅ Core da demo (What-If + Calendar) já existe
- ✅ Dados realistas (128 trens Dubai)
- ✅ UI profissional (Cloudscape)
- ✅ Código MongoDB existe

**O que precisa ser feito:**
1. **Excel Export** - OBRIGATÓRIO para demo
2. **MongoDB Connection** - CRÍTICO para persistência
3. **Testar fluxo end-to-end** - Antes da demo

**Decisão necessária:**
- Demo com dados em memória (mais rápido, menos completo)?
- Ou investir 2 dias para MongoDB completo?

---

**Próximos passos - o que você quer que eu faça?**

1. Implementar Excel Export agora?
2. Configurar e conectar MongoDB?
3. Verificar/testar o que já existe antes de adicionar mais?
4. Outra prioridade?

Based on my search results, here's the latest information on Astro JS and getting started:

## Astro JS Latest Version

The latest release of Astro is v5.16.4.(no content)It was last published about 6 days ago.

## Getting Started with Astro

### Prerequisites
You need Node.js - v18.20.8 or v20.3.0, v22.0.0 or higher. (v19 and v21 are not supported.)(no content)A text editor is recommended - VS Code with the Official Astro extension. Astro is accessed through its command-line interface (CLI).

### Installation

The recommended way to install Astro is:

The recommended command is `npm create astro@latest`. You can also manually install with `npm install --save-dev astro`.(no content)You can run create astro anywhere on your machine, so there's no need to create a new empty directory for your project before you begin. If you don't have an empty directory yet for your new project, the wizard will help create one.

### Resources

- The official documentation provides guides, resources, and API references to help you build with Astro — the web framework for content-driven websites.
- You can complete the introductory Build a Blog Tutorial to learn the basics and create your first Astro site.
- You can try Astro in your browser by visiting astro.new to browse starter templates and spin up a new Astro project without ever leaving your browser.

### Key Links
- **Official Documentation**: https://docs.astro.build/en/getting-started/
- **Installation Guide**: https://docs.astro.build/en/install-and-setup/
- **GitHub Repository**: https://github.com/withastro/astro
- **NPM Package**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/astro
- **Try in Browser**: https://astro.new/latest/

Based on the search results, here's the latest information about Palantir's Blueprint React UI library:

## Blueprint - Palantir's React UI Library

Blueprint is an open source project developed at Palantir. It's a React-based UI toolkit for the web.

### Latest Version: Blueprint 6.0

Blueprint 6.0 is now available (as of June 2025). It focuses on the removal of deprecated and legacy code and the modernization of the codebase.

### Key Changes in Blueprint 6.0:

- CSS namespace changed from bp5- to bp6-.
- React 16 and 17 are no longer supported as peer dependencies of Blueprint.
- Blueprint no longer produces UMD bundles from its packages.
- The showContextMenu function now only accepts ShowContextMenuOptions for its second argument and uses the React 18 ReactDOMClient.createRoot async API instead of the React 16 ReactDOM.render by default.
- The react-day-picker dependency has changed from 7.4.9 to ^8.10.0.
- Switched out the implementation for deprecated components. With each of these components the versioned component has replaced the unversioned variant. For example, the previously deprecated `<PanelStack>` component is now implemented using the `<PanelStack2>` implementation and API.

### Blueprint 5.x (Previous Major Version)

The 5.x series included significant updates such as:
- Size prop improvements: Across the board, components that can render at different size scales have a new size prop with possible values of "medium" (default), "small", and "large". The existing small and large props on components are now deprecated.
- Upgraded internal dependencies to React 18, updating component types and adding deprecations.

### Key Features:
- Blueprint provides date components like date picker, range picker, and time picker that lets developers build time and date interactivity.
- Blueprint UI supports internationalization, making building applications for different regions and languages easy. Utilizing the class .bp5-rtl, languages like Hebrew and Arabic are written from right to left.
- Blueprint UI provides two UI color themes: light and dark.

**Official Resources:**
- GitHub: https://github.com/palantir/blueprint
- Documentation: https://blueprintjs.com/

Here are the search results for "Astro React integration islands architecture 2024":

## Astro React Integration and Islands Architecture

### What is Islands Architecture?

Astro helped pioneer and popularize a new frontend architecture pattern called Islands Architecture. Islands architecture works by rendering the majority of your page to fast, static HTML with smaller "islands" of JavaScript added when interactivity or personalization is needed on the page (an image carousel, for example). This avoids the monolithic JavaScript payloads that slow down the responsiveness of many other, modern JavaScript web frameworks.(no content)The term "component island" was first coined by Etsy's frontend architect Katie Sylor-Miller in 2019. This idea was then expanded on and documented by Preact creator Jason Miller on August 11, 2020.

### How Islands Work in Astro

## Blueprint JS 6.0 Overview

Blueprint is a React UI toolkit for the web. The @blueprintjs/datetime package contains a collection of React components for working with dates and times.

### DateTime Components

## Blueprint.js NPM Packages Overview

Blueprint publishes component libraries to NPM. The main packages include:

### @blueprintjs/core
- Core styles & components
- Version 6.1.0 was published 25 days ago

### @blueprintjs/datetime
- This package contains a collection of React components for working with dates and times.
- Latest version is 6.0.5, last published about a month ago.
- There are 97 other projects in the npm registry using @blueprintjs/datetime.
- react-day-picker v8 is a dependency of this package; they use it to render calendars.
- The package receives about 27,898 weekly downloads and is classified as popular.

### @blueprintjs/table
- Scalable & interactive spreadsheet-like table component

## Blueprint 6.0 Updates (2025)

Blueprint 6.0 is now available (as of June 2025). It focuses on the removal of deprecated and legacy code and the modernization of the codebase.

Key changes:
- CSS namespace changed from bp5- to bp6-.
- React 16 and 17 are no longer supported as peer dependencies of Blueprint.
- @blueprintjs/datetime2 is now deprecated and should no longer be used. All component implementations have been merged into @blueprintjs/datetime.
- The react-day-picker dependency has changed from 7.4.9 to ^8.10.0.

### Development Requirements
- Prerequisites: Node.js v20.11+ (see version specified in .nvmrc), pnpm v10.x (see version specified in package.json)

Based on the search results, here's what I found about setting up Astro with React integration:

## Creating an Astro Project with React Integration

### Step 1: Create a New Astro Project
Run the command `npm create astro@latest` to start the setup wizard. After running the command, Astro's project creation wizard will guide you through the setup process. You'll be prompted to choose a project directory and select a project template.

### Step 2: Add React Integration
The Astro React integration enables rendering and client-side hydration for your React components. Astro includes an `astro add` command to automate the setup of official integrations.(no content)Add the React integration using the astro add command for your package manager. If your app uses other packages supported by the astro add command, like Tailwind and MDX, you can add them all with one command.

Run:
```bash
npx astro add react
```

The above command will install React, set up the necessary dependencies and configure Astro to use React components.

### Step 3: Configuration
Your `astro.config.mjs` should include:
```javascript
import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config';
import react from '@astrojs/react';

export default defineConfig({
  integrations: [react()],
});
```

### Using React Components
React components live inside the `src/components` folder of an Astro project.(no content)

input.content:
# Migration Plan: Astro + Blueprint JS

**Date:** December 10, 2025
**Status:** IN PROGRESS
**Objective:** Create a new, working frontend app replacing the broken React Router apps

---

## 1. SITUAÇÃO ATUAL

### Apps Quebrados

| App | Problema | Status |
|-----|----------|--------|
| `apps/mms/` | TypeScript errors, React Router types não resolvem | ❌ Não roda |
| `apps/mms-old/` | Migração incompleta | ❌ Não roda |

### O Que Funciona

| Componente | Status | Localização |
|------------|--------|-------------|
| Python Solver | ✅ 16/16 testes | `packages/solver/` |
| API Server | ✅ Funciona | `packages/api/` |
| Types Package | ✅ Completo | `packages/types/` |
| Demo Data | ✅ Útil | `apps/mms-old/app/lib/demo-data.ts` |
| Export Utils | ✅ Excel + CSV | `apps/mms-old/app/lib/export-utils.ts` |

### Código Reutilizável

1. **`demo-data.ts`** - Gerador de dados realistas (79 Alstom + 49 Kinki)
2. **`export-utils.ts`** - Excel export completo (6 abas) + Maximo CSV
3. **`api.ts`** - Cliente API para solver
4. **`@uptime/types`** - Todas as interfaces TypeScript
5. **Lógica dos componentes** - What-If, Calendar, Dashboard (adaptar UI)

---

## 2. NOVA STACK TECNOLÓGICA

### Frontend

| Tecnologia | Versão | Justificativa |
|------------|--------|---------------|
| **Astro** | 5.x (latest) | Simples, rápido, islands architecture |
| **React** | 18.x | Requerido pelo Blueprint |
| **Blueprint JS** | 6.x | UI enterprise, completa, bem documentada |
| **TypeScript** | 5.x | Type safety |

### Blueprint Packages

```
@blueprintjs/core        - Componentes base (Button, Card, Dialog, etc.)
@blueprintjs/icons       - Ícones
@blueprintjs/select      - Select, MultiSelect, Suggest
@blueprintjs/table       - Tabelas interativas
@blueprintjs/datetime    - DatePicker, TimePicker
```

### Backend (sem mudanças)

- API: Hono + Bun (já funciona)
- Solver: Python + HiGHS (já funciona)
- Database: MongoDB (a conectar)

---

## 3. ESTRUTURA DO NOVO APP

```
apps/web/                          # Novo app Astro
├── astro.config.mjs               # Config Astro + React
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── public/
│   └── favicon.svg
└── src/
    ├── layouts/
    │   └── MainLayout.astro       # Layout com sidebar Blueprint
    ├── pages/
    │   ├── index.astro            # Dashboard
    │   ├── fleet.astro            # Fleet Management
    │   ├── schedule.astro         # Schedule Table
    │   ├── calendar.astro         # Calendar View
    │   └── what-if.astro          # What-If Simulator
    ├── components/
    │   ├── Sidebar.tsx            # React - Blueprint NavBar
    │   ├── Dashboard.tsx          # React - KPIs e charts
    │   ├── FleetTable.tsx         # React - Blueprint Table
    │   ├── ScheduleTable.tsx      # React - Blueprint Table
    │   ├── CalendarView.tsx       # React - Calendar custom
    │   ├── WhatIfSimulator.tsx    # React - Core demo
    │   └── WorkloadChart.tsx      # React - Charts
    ├── lib/
    │   ├── api.ts                 # API client (copiar)
    │   ├── demo-data.ts           # Data generator (copiar)
    │   └── export-utils.ts        # Excel/CSV (copiar)
    └── styles/
        └── global.css             # Blueprint CSS imports
```

---

## 4. PLANO DE EXECUÇÃO

### Fase 1: Setup (30 min)

1. Criar app Astro com pnpm
2. Adicionar integração React
3. Instalar Blueprint packages
4. Configurar CSS do Blueprint
5. Verificar que roda

### Fase 2: Layout Base (1h)

1. Criar `MainLayout.astro` com estrutura básica
2. Criar `Sidebar.tsx` com Blueprint Navbar
3. Criar páginas vazias (index, fleet, schedule, calendar, what-if)
4. Verificar navegação funciona

### Fase 3: Migrar Código Útil (30 min)

1. Copiar `demo-data.ts` para `src/lib/`
2. Copiar `export-utils.ts` para `src/lib/`
3. Copiar `api.ts` para `src/lib/`
4. Ajustar imports se necessário

### Fase 4: What-If Simulator (2h) - PRIORIDADE

1. Criar `WhatIfSimulator.tsx` com Blueprint
   - Form com sliders e selects
   - Botão Run Simulation
   - Resultado com métricas
2. Criar `WorkloadChart.tsx` para visualização
3. Integrar com API do solver
4. Testar end-to-end

### Fase 5: Calendar View (1.5h)

1. Criar `CalendarView.tsx` com Blueprint
   - Navegação por mês
   - Visualização semanal
   - Modal de detalhes
2. Integrar com dados do solver

### Fase 6: Dashboard (1h)

1. Criar `Dashboard.tsx` com KPIs
   - Cards com métricas
   - Resumo da frota
2. Integrar com dados

### Fase 7: Fleet & Schedule Tables (1h)

1. `FleetTable.tsx` - Blueprint Table com 128 trens
2. `ScheduleTable.tsx` - Blueprint Table com eventos

### Fase 8: Export Functions (30 min)

1. Conectar botões de export ao `export-utils.ts`
2. Testar download Excel
3. Testar download CSV Maximo

---

## 5. COMANDOS DE SETUP

```bash
# 1. Criar novo app Astro
cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/uptime/apps
pnpm create astro@latest web -- --template minimal --typescript strict

# 2. Adicionar React
cd web
pnpm astro add react

# 3. Instalar Blueprint
pnpm add @blueprintjs/core @blueprintjs/icons @blueprintjs/select @blueprintjs/table @blueprintjs/datetime

# 4. Instalar dependências extras
pnpm add xlsx                    # Para export Excel
pnpm add @uptime/types@workspace:*  # Types compartilhados

# 5. Dev server
pnpm dev
```

---

## 6. BLUEPRINT QUICK REFERENCE

### CSS Import (global.css)

```css
@import "@blueprintjs/core/lib/css/blueprint.css";
@import "@blueprintjs/icons/lib/css/blueprint-icons.css";
@import "@blueprintjs/table/lib/css/table.css";
@import "@blueprintjs/datetime/lib/css/blueprint-datetime.css";
```

### Componentes Principais

```tsx
// Layout
import { Navbar, NavbarGroup, NavbarHeading, NavbarDivider } from "@blueprintjs/core";
import { Card, Elevation } from "@blueprintjs/core";

// Forms
import { Button, Intent } from "@blueprintjs/core";
import { Slider, NumericInput } from "@blueprintjs/core";
import { Select } from "@blueprintjs/select";

// Data
import { Table2, Column, Cell } from "@blueprintjs/table";
import { Tag, ProgressBar } from "@blueprintjs/core";

// Feedback
import { Spinner, NonIdealState } from "@blueprintjs/core";
import { Toaster, Toast } from "@blueprintjs/core";
```

### Dark Mode

```tsx
// Adicionar classe ao body
document.body.classList.add("bp5-dark");
```

---

## 7. RISCOS E MITIGAÇÕES

| Risco | Probabilidade | Mitigação |
|-------|---------------|-----------|
| Blueprint não funciona com React 18 | Baixa | Blueprint 6.0 suporta React 18 |
| Astro + React issues | Média | Islands architecture é bem documentada |
| Tempo insuficiente | Alta | Focar no What-If primeiro (core da demo) |
| Charts no Blueprint | Média | Blueprint não tem charts nativos - usar recharts ou similar |

---

## 8. ORDEM DE PRIORIDADE PARA DEMO

1. **What-If Simulator** - Core da demo, prova que solver funciona
2. **Calendar View** - Validação visual do schedule
3. **Excel Export** - Cliente precisa para validar offline
4. Dashboard - Nice to have
5. Fleet/Schedule Tables - Nice to have

---

## 9. DEFINIÇÃO DE SUCESSO

A demo está pronta quando:

- [ ] App roda sem erros (`pnpm dev`)
- [ ] What-If Simulator executa solver e mostra resultados
- [ ] Calendar View mostra schedule gerado
- [ ] Excel Export baixa arquivo com 6 abas
- [ ] UI é profissional e responsiva

---

*Documento criado: December 10, 2025*

# Migration Plan: Astro + Blueprint JS

**Date:** December 10, 2025
**Status:** IN PROGRESS
**Objective:** Create a new, working frontend app replacing the broken React Router apps

---

## 1. SITUAÇÃO ATUAL

### Apps Quebrados

| App | Problema | Status |
|-----|----------|--------|
| `apps/mms/` | TypeScript errors, React Router types não resolvem | ❌ Não roda |
| `apps/mms-old/` | Migração incompleta | ❌ Não roda |

### O Que Supostamente Funciona (REQUER REVALIDAÇÃO)

| Componente | Status Alegado | Localização | Validado? |
|------------|----------------|-------------|-----------|
| Python Solver | 16/16 testes | `packages/solver/` | ✅ Sim - testes passam |
| API Server | Funciona | `packages/api/` | ❌ Não testado em runtime |
| Types Package | Completo | `packages/types/` | ❌ Não validado |
| Demo Data | Útil | `apps/mms-old/app/lib/demo-data.ts` | ❌ Não validado |
| Export Utils | Excel + CSV | `apps/mms-old/app/lib/export-utils.ts` | ❌ Não validado |

**⚠️ IMPORTANTE:** Apenas o solver foi validado executando testes reais. Todo o resto precisa ser verificado antes de assumir que funciona.

### Código Potencialmente Reutilizável

1. **`demo-data.ts`** - Gerador de dados realistas (79 Alstom + 49 Kinki)
2. **`export-utils.ts`** - Excel export (6 abas) + Maximo CSV
3. **`api.ts`** - Cliente API para solver
4. **`@uptime/types`** - Interfaces TypeScript
5. **Lógica dos componentes** - What-If, Calendar, Dashboard (adaptar UI)

---

## 2. NOVA STACK TECNOLÓGICA

### Frontend

| Tecnologia | Versão | Justificativa |
|------------|--------|---------------|
| **Astro** | 5.x (latest) | Simples, rápido, islands architecture |
| **React** | 18.x | Requerido pelo Blueprint 6.x |
| **Blueprint JS** | 6.x | UI enterprise, completa, bem documentada |
| **TypeScript** | 5.x | Type safety |

### Blueprint Packages

```
@blueprintjs/core        - Componentes base (Button, Card, Dialog, etc.)
@blueprintjs/icons       - Ícones
@blueprintjs/select      - Select, MultiSelect, Suggest
@blueprintjs/table       - Tabelas interativas
@blueprintjs/datetime    - DatePicker, TimePicker
```

### Backend (sem mudanças planejadas)

- API: Hono + Bun
- Solver: Python + HiGHS
- Database: MongoDB (a conectar)

---

## 3. ESTRUTURA DO NOVO APP

```
apps/web/                          # Novo app Astro
├── astro.config.mjs               # Config Astro + React
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── public/
│   └── favicon.svg
└── src/
    ├── layouts/
    │   └── MainLayout.astro       # Layout com sidebar Blueprint
    ├── pages/
    │   ├── index.astro            # Dashboard
    │   ├── fleet.astro            # Fleet Management
    │   ├── schedule.astro         # Schedule Table
    │   ├── calendar.astro         # Calendar View
    │   └── what-if.astro          # What-If Simulator
    ├── components/
    │   ├── Sidebar.tsx            # React - Blueprint NavBar
    │   ├── Dashboard.tsx          # React - KPIs e charts
    │   ├── FleetTable.tsx         # React - Blueprint Table
    │   ├── ScheduleTable.tsx      # React - Blueprint Table
    │   ├── CalendarView.tsx       # React - Calendar custom
    │   ├── WhatIfSimulator.tsx    # React - Core demo
    │   └── WorkloadChart.tsx      # React - Charts
    ├── lib/
    │   ├── api.ts                 # API client (copiar e validar)
    │   ├── demo-data.ts           # Data generator (copiar e validar)
    │   └── export-utils.ts        # Excel/CSV (copiar e validar)
    └── styles/
        └── global.css             # Blueprint CSS imports
```

---

## 4. PLANO DE EXECUÇÃO

### Fase 1: Setup

1. Criar app Astro com pnpm
2. Adicionar integração React
3. Instalar Blueprint packages
4. Configurar CSS do Blueprint
5. **Verificar que roda** (não assumir)

### Fase 2: Layout Base

1. Criar `MainLayout.astro` com estrutura básica
2. Criar `Sidebar.tsx` com Blueprint Navbar
3. Criar páginas vazias (index, fleet, schedule, calendar, what-if)
4. **Verificar navegação funciona** (não assumir)

### Fase 3: Migrar e Validar Código

1. Copiar `demo-data.ts` para `src/lib/`
2. Copiar `export-utils.ts` para `src/lib/`
3. Copiar `api.ts` para `src/lib/`
4. **Testar cada arquivo isoladamente** antes de integrar
5. Ajustar imports se necessário

### Fase 4: What-If Simulator - PRIORIDADE MÁXIMA

1. Criar `WhatIfSimulator.tsx` com Blueprint
   - Form com sliders e selects
   - Botão Run Simulation
   - Resultado com métricas
2. Criar `WorkloadChart.tsx` para visualização
3. Integrar com API do solver
4. **Testar end-to-end com solver real**

### Fase 5: Calendar View

1. Criar `CalendarView.tsx` com Blueprint
   - Navegação por mês
   - Visualização semanal
   - Modal de detalhes
2. Integrar com dados do solver
3. **Validar que dados aparecem corretamente**

### Fase 6: Dashboard

1. Criar `Dashboard.tsx` com KPIs
   - Cards com métricas
   - Resumo da frota
2. Integrar com dados

### Fase 7: Fleet & Schedule Tables

1. `FleetTable.tsx` - Blueprint Table com 128 trens
2. `ScheduleTable.tsx` - Blueprint Table com eventos

### Fase 8: Export Functions

1. Conectar botões de export ao `export-utils.ts`
2. **Testar download Excel** - abrir arquivo e verificar conteúdo
3. **Testar download CSV Maximo** - verificar formato

---

## 5. COMANDOS DE SETUP

```bash
# 1. Criar novo app Astro
cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/uptime/apps
pnpm create astro@latest web -- --template minimal --typescript strict

# 2. Adicionar React
cd web
pnpm astro add react

# 3. Instalar Blueprint
pnpm add @blueprintjs/core @blueprintjs/icons @blueprintjs/select @blueprintjs/table @blueprintjs/datetime

# 4. Instalar dependências extras
pnpm add xlsx                    # Para export Excel
pnpm add @uptime/types@workspace:*  # Types compartilhados

# 5. Dev server
pnpm dev
```

---

## 6. BLUEPRINT QUICK REFERENCE

### CSS Import (global.css)

```css
@import "@blueprintjs/core/lib/css/blueprint.css";
@import "@blueprintjs/icons/lib/css/blueprint-icons.css";
@import "@blueprintjs/table/lib/css/table.css";
@import "@blueprintjs/datetime/lib/css/blueprint-datetime.css";
```

### Componentes Principais

```tsx
// Layout
import { Navbar, NavbarGroup, NavbarHeading, NavbarDivider } from "@blueprintjs/core";
import { Card, Elevation } from "@blueprintjs/core";

// Forms
import { Button, Intent } from "@blueprintjs/core";
import { Slider, NumericInput } from "@blueprintjs/core";
import { Select } from "@blueprintjs/select";

// Data
import { Table2, Column, Cell } from "@blueprintjs/table";
import { Tag, ProgressBar } from "@blueprintjs/core";

// Feedback
import { Spinner, NonIdealState } from "@blueprintjs/core";
import { Toaster, Toast } from "@blueprintjs/core";
```

### Dark Mode

```tsx
// Adicionar classe ao body
document.body.classList.add("bp6-dark");
```

---

## 7. RISCOS E MITIGAÇÕES

| Risco | Mitigação |
|-------|-----------|
| Blueprint não funciona com React 18 | Blueprint 6.0 suporta React 18 (verificar docs) |
| Astro + React issues | Islands architecture é bem documentada |
| Charts no Blueprint | Blueprint não tem charts nativos - avaliar recharts ou similar |
| Código antigo não funciona | Testar cada parte antes de integrar |
| API não conecta ao solver | Validar API server funciona antes de integrar frontend |

---

## 8. ORDEM DE PRIORIDADE PARA DEMO

1. **What-If Simulator** - Core da demo, prova que solver funciona
2. **Calendar View** - Validação visual do schedule
3. **Excel Export** - Cliente precisa para validar offline
4. Dashboard - Secundário
5. Fleet/Schedule Tables - Secundário

---

## 9. CHECKLIST DE VALIDAÇÃO

A demo está pronta quando TODOS os itens forem verificados manualmente:

- [ ] App roda sem erros (`pnpm dev`)
- [ ] Navegação entre páginas funciona
- [ ] What-If Simulator executa solver e mostra resultados
- [ ] Solver retorna dados válidos (não mock)
- [ ] Calendar View mostra schedule gerado pelo solver
- [ ] Excel Export baixa arquivo
- [ ] Excel abre corretamente e tem 6 abas com dados
- [ ] UI é profissional e responsiva

---

## 10. O QUE NÃO ASSUMIR

- ❌ Não assumir que código copiado funciona - testar
- ❌ Não assumir que dependências instalam sem erro - verificar
- ❌ Não assumir que API funciona - testar endpoint
- ❌ Não assumir que tipos estão corretos - validar em runtime
- ❌ Não assumir que export gera arquivo válido - abrir e verificar

---

*Documento criado: December 10, 2025*

---

## 9. CHECKLIST DE VALIDAÇÃO

A demo está pronta quando TODOS os itens forem verificados manualmente:

- [ ] App roda sem erros (`pnpm dev`)
- [ ] Navegação entre páginas funciona
- [ ] What-If Simulator executa solver e mostra resultados
- [ ] Solver retorna dados válidos (não mock)
- [ ] Calendar View mostra schedule gerado pelo solver
- [ ] Excel Export baixa arquivo
- [ ] Excel abre corretamente e tem 6 abas com dados
- [ ] UI é profissional e responsiva

---

## 10. O QUE NÃO ASSUMIR

- ❌ Não assumir que código copiado funciona - testar
- ❌ Não assumir que dependências instalam sem erro - verificar
- ❌ Não assumir que API funciona - testar endpoint
- ❌ Não assumir que tipos estão corretos - validar em runtime
- ❌ Não assumir que export gera arquivo válido - abrir e verificar

---

*Documento criado: December 10, 2025*

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Blueprint JS and React 19 compatibility:

## Blueprint JS React 19 Compatibility Status

### Current Situation

There is an open feature request (Issue #7115) on the Blueprint GitHub repository to add support for React 19. Users want to leverage React 19 in their apps that use Blueprint to benefit from the React compiler and other performance improvements.

### Key Blocker: React-Popper Deprecation

As of December 4, 2024, React-Popper is deprecated, with no direct recommendation for replacement (but it hints at using floating-ui). This deprecation blocks @blueprintjs/core from fully supporting React 19, since the library is primarily used in the Popover component.(no content)Together with breaking changes in React 19 regarding Refs, the behavior of React-Popper is unstable on React 19. This can be validated by testing the Select component, which fails to detect proper bounding boxes and renders the dropdown menu outside the component, appearing instead on the corner of the window.

### Latest Blueprint Version

The latest version of @blueprintjs/core is 6.1.0, published about 25 days ago.

### Internal React 18 Usage

Blueprint has upgraded internal dependencies to React 18, updating component types and adding deprecations, with no expected impact on downstream consumers.

### Summary

Blueprint JS does **not yet fully support React 19**. The main blocking issue is the dependency on the deprecated react-popper library, which has compatibility issues with React 19's ref changes. Users experiencing issues with Popover-based components (like Select) when using React 19 should be aware of these limitations. The Blueprint team will likely need to migrate to floating-ui or a similar alternative before full React 19 support can be achieved.

content.text:
**Problema identificado:** Blueprint JS ainda **NÃO suporta React 19** completamente. Há um [issue aberto #7115](https://github.com/palantir/blueprint/issues/7115) sobre isso. O bloqueio principal é o `react-popper` que está deprecado e tem problemas com React 19.

**Opções:**

1. **Usar React 18** com Blueprint (garantido funcionar)
2. **Usar outra UI library** que suporte React 19
3. **Tentar Blueprint com React 19** e ver se funciona para nossos casos de uso (arriscado)

O que você prefere? Ou quer que eu pesquise alternativas ao Blueprint que suportem React 19?

content.content:
# React Peer Dependency for Blueprint

Based on the package.json file shown, Blueprint specifies the following React versions:

- **@types/react**: `"^18.3.12"`
- **@types/react-dom**: `"^18.3.1"`

The `^18.3.12` notation indicates Blueprint supports React 18.3.12 and compatible versions within the 18.x range. This means the library works with React 18.3.12 and later minor/patch updates, but not with React 19 or major version changes.

# Migration Plan: Astro + Blueprint JS

**Date:** December 10, 2025
**Status:** IN PROGRESS
**Objective:** Create a new, working frontend app replacing the broken React Router apps

---

## 1. SITUAÇÃO ATUAL

### Apps Quebrados

| App | Problema | Status |
|-----|----------|--------|
| `apps/mms/` | TypeScript errors, React Router types não resolvem | ❌ Não roda |
| `apps/mms-old/` | Migração incompleta | ❌ Não roda |

### O Que Supostamente Funciona (REQUER REVALIDAÇÃO)

| Componente | Status Alegado | Localização | Validado? |
|------------|----------------|-------------|-----------|
| Python Solver | 16/16 testes | `packages/solver/` | ✅ Sim - testes passam |
| API Server | Funciona | `packages/api/` | ❌ Não testado em runtime |
| Types Package | Completo | `packages/types/` | ❌ Não validado |
| Demo Data | Útil | `apps/mms-old/app/lib/demo-data.ts` | ❌ Não validado |
| Export Utils | Excel + CSV | `apps/mms-old/app/lib/export-utils.ts` | ❌ Não validado |

**⚠️ IMPORTANTE:** Apenas o solver foi validado executando testes reais. Todo o resto precisa ser verificado antes de assumir que funciona.

### Código Potencialmente Reutilizável

1. **`demo-data.ts`** - Gerador de dados realistas (79 Alstom + 49 Kinki)
2. **`export-utils.ts`** - Excel export (6 abas) + Maximo CSV
3. **`api.ts`** - Cliente API para solver
4. **`@uptime/types`** - Interfaces TypeScript
5. **Lógica dos componentes** - What-If, Calendar, Dashboard (adaptar UI)

---

## 2. NOVA STACK TECNOLÓGICA

### Frontend

| Tecnologia | Versão | Justificativa |
|------------|--------|---------------|
| **Astro** | 5.x (latest) | Simples, rápido, islands architecture |
| **React** | 18.x | Requerido pelo Blueprint 6.x |
| **Blueprint JS** | 6.x | UI enterprise, completa, bem documentada |
| **TypeScript** | 5.x | Type safety |

### Blueprint Packages

```
@blueprintjs/core        - Componentes base (Button, Card, Dialog, etc.)
@blueprintjs/icons       - Ícones
@blueprintjs/select      - Select, MultiSelect, Suggest
@blueprintjs/table       - Tabelas interativas
@blueprintjs/datetime    - DatePicker, TimePicker
```

### Backend (sem mudanças planejadas)

- API: Hono + Bun
- Solver: Python + HiGHS
- Database: MongoDB (a conectar)

---

## 3. ESTRUTURA DO NOVO APP

```
apps/web/                          # Novo app Astro
├── astro.config.mjs               # Config Astro + React
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── public/
│   └── favicon.svg
└── src/
    ├── layouts/
    │   └── MainLayout.astro       # Layout com sidebar Blueprint
    ├── pages/
    │   ├── index.astro            # Dashboard
    │   ├── fleet.astro            # Fleet Management
    │   ├── schedule.astro         # Schedule Table
    │   ├── calendar.astro         # Calendar View
    │   └── what-if.astro          # What-If Simulator
    ├── components/
    │   ├── Sidebar.tsx            # React - Blueprint NavBar
    │   ├── Dashboard.tsx          # React - KPIs e charts
    │   ├── FleetTable.tsx         # React - Blueprint Table
    │   ├── ScheduleTable.tsx      # React - Blueprint Table
    │   ├── CalendarView.tsx       # React - Calendar custom
    │   ├── WhatIfSimulator.tsx    # React - Core demo
    │   └── WorkloadChart.tsx      # React - Charts
    ├── lib/
    │   ├── api.ts                 # API client (copiar e validar)
    │   ├── demo-data.ts           # Data generator (copiar e validar)
    │   └── export-utils.ts        # Excel/CSV (copiar e validar)
    └── styles/
        └── global.css             # Blueprint CSS imports
```

---

## 4. PLANO DE EXECUÇÃO

### Fase 1: Setup

1. Criar app Astro com pnpm
2. Adicionar integração React
3. Instalar Blueprint packages
4. Configurar CSS do Blueprint
5. **Verificar que roda** (não assumir)

### Fase 2: Layout Base

1. Criar `MainLayout.astro` com estrutura básica
2. Criar `Sidebar.tsx` com Blueprint Navbar
3. Criar páginas vazias (index, fleet, schedule, calendar, what-if)
4. **Verificar navegação funciona** (não assumir)

### Fase 3: Migrar e Validar Código

1. Copiar `demo-data.ts` para `src/lib/`
2. Copiar `export-utils.ts` para `src/lib/`
3. Copiar `api.ts` para `src/lib/`
4. **Testar cada arquivo isoladamente** antes de integrar
5. Ajustar imports se necessário

### Fase 4: What-If Simulator - PRIORIDADE MÁXIMA

1. Criar `WhatIfSimulator.tsx` com Blueprint
   - Form com sliders e selects
   - Botão Run Simulation
   - Resultado com métricas
2. Criar `WorkloadChart.tsx` para visualização
3. Integrar com API do solver
4. **Testar end-to-end com solver real**

### Fase 5: Calendar View

1. Criar `CalendarView.tsx` com Blueprint
   - Navegação por mês
   - Visualização semanal
   - Modal de detalhes
2. Integrar com dados do solver
3. **Validar que dados aparecem corretamente**

### Fase 6: Dashboard

1. Criar `Dashboard.tsx` com KPIs
   - Cards com métricas
   - Resumo da frota
2. Integrar com dados

### Fase 7: Fleet & Schedule Tables

1. `FleetTable.tsx` - Blueprint Table com 128 trens
2. `ScheduleTable.tsx` - Blueprint Table com eventos

### Fase 8: Export Functions

1. Conectar botões de export ao `export-utils.ts`
2. **Testar download Excel** - abrir arquivo e verificar conteúdo
3. **Testar download CSV Maximo** - verificar formato

---

## 5. COMANDOS DE SETUP

```bash
# 1. Criar novo app Astro
cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/uptime/apps
pnpm create astro@latest web -- --template minimal --typescript strict

# 2. Adicionar React
cd web
pnpm astro add react

# 3. Instalar Blueprint
pnpm add @blueprintjs/core @blueprintjs/icons @blueprintjs/select @blueprintjs/table @blueprintjs/datetime

# 4. Instalar dependências extras
pnpm add xlsx                    # Para export Excel
pnpm add @uptime/types@workspace:*  # Types compartilhados

# 5. Dev server
pnpm dev
```

---

## 6. BLUEPRINT QUICK REFERENCE

### CSS Import (global.css)

```css
@import "@blueprintjs/core/lib/css/blueprint.css";
@import "@blueprintjs/icons/lib/css/blueprint-icons.css";
@import "@blueprintjs/table/lib/css/table.css";
@import "@blueprintjs/datetime/lib/css/blueprint-datetime.css";
```

### Componentes Principais

```tsx
// Layout
import { Navbar, NavbarGroup, NavbarHeading, NavbarDivider } from "@blueprintjs/core";
import { Card, Elevation } from "@blueprintjs/core";

// Forms
import { Button, Intent } from "@blueprintjs/core";
import { Slider, NumericInput } from "@blueprintjs/core";
import { Select } from "@blueprintjs/select";

// Data
import { Table2, Column, Cell } from "@blueprintjs/table";
import { Tag, ProgressBar } from "@blueprintjs/core";

// Feedback
import { Spinner, NonIdealState } from "@blueprintjs/core";
import { Toaster, Toast } from "@blueprintjs/core";
```

### Dark Mode

```tsx
// Adicionar classe ao body
document.body.classList.add("bp6-dark");
```

---

## 7. RISCOS E MITIGAÇÕES

| Risco | Mitigação |
|-------|-----------|
| Blueprint não funciona com React 18 | Blueprint 6.0 suporta React 18 (verificar docs) |
| Astro + React issues | Islands architecture é bem documentada |
| Charts no Blueprint | Blueprint não tem charts nativos - avaliar recharts ou similar |
| Código antigo não funciona | Testar cada parte antes de integrar |
| API não conecta ao solver | Validar API server funciona antes de integrar frontend |

---

## 8. ORDEM DE PRIORIDADE PARA DEMO

1. **What-If Simulator** - Core da demo, prova que solver funciona
2. **Calendar View** - Validação visual do schedule
3. **Excel Export** - Cliente precisa para validar offline
4. Dashboard - Secundário
5. Fleet/Schedule Tables - Secundário

---

## 9. CHECKLIST DE VALIDAÇÃO

A demo está pronta quando TODOS os itens forem verificados manualmente:

- [ ] App roda sem erros (`pnpm dev`)
- [ ] Navegação entre páginas funciona
- [ ] What-If Simulator executa solver e mostra resultados
- [ ] Solver retorna dados válidos (não mock)
- [ ] Calendar View mostra schedule gerado pelo solver
- [ ] Excel Export baixa arquivo
- [ ] Excel abre corretamente e tem 6 abas com dados
- [ ] UI é profissional e responsiva

---

## 10. O QUE NÃO ASSUMIR

- ❌ Não assumir que código copiado funciona - testar
- ❌ Não assumir que dependências instalam sem erro - verificar
- ❌ Não assumir que API funciona - testar endpoint
- ❌ Não assumir que tipos estão corretos - validar em runtime
- ❌ Não assumir que export gera arquivo válido - abrir e verificar

---

*Documento criado: December 10, 2025*

diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
index 30ca792..4625d7c 100644
--- a/CLAUDE.md
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -1,5 +1,11 @@
 # Dubai Metro MMS - AI Instructions
 
+## ⚠️ CRITICAL PROJECT STATUS
+
+**THIS PROJECT IS AT RISK.** Much of the documentation and code was AI-generated without proper verification. The SoW sent to client caused confusion. We must rebuild trust through working software, not promises.
+
+**NOTHING IS ASSUMED WORKING UNTIL MANUALLY VERIFIED.**
+
 ## Project Overview
 
 **Mileage Management System (MMS)** for **Keolis MHI** - Dubai Metro operator.
@@ -10,19 +16,21 @@
 | Fleet         | 128 trains (79 Alstom + 49 Kinki Sharyo) |
 | Contract      | 12-year horizon                          |
 | Value         | $147,500                                 |
-| Current Phase | Pre-sales / Demo Recovery                |
+| Current Phase | **CRITICAL - Demo Recovery**             |
 
 ## Source of Truth
 
-**TRUST ONLY:**
+**TRUST ONLY THESE TWO DOCUMENTS:**
 
-1. `docs/@start-here/RFQ.md` - Client requirements (extracted from PDF)
-2. `docs/@start-here/proposal_and_sow.md` - Contract commitment (extracted from PDF)
+1. `docs/@start-here/RFQ.md` - Client requirements (from client - THIS IS THE GUIDE)
+2. `docs/@start-here/proposal_and_sow.md` - Our commitment (problematic but already sent)
 
-**Key References:**
+**⚠️ WARNING about other documentation:**
 
-- `docs/knowledge-base/` - Domain knowledge, architecture, implementation guides
-- `packages/solver/` - Working solver with tests
+- `docs/knowledge-base/` - AI-generated, needs verification against RFQ
+- `docs/internal/` - AI-generated, may contain hallucinations
+- Code comments and READMEs - AI-generated, may not reflect reality
+- **Test counts, performance claims - MUST BE RE-VERIFIED**
 
 ## Context Strategy
 
@@ -140,15 +148,24 @@ pnpm gen:context      # Generate AI context (repomix)
 
 ## Current State (Dec 2025)
 
-**Completed:**
+**⚠️ UNVERIFIED - All claims below need manual verification:**
+
+| Component | Claimed Status | Verified? |
+|-----------|---------------|-----------|
+| Python Solver | "20 passing tests" | ❌ NOT VERIFIED |
+| API Server | "14/14 tests" | ❌ NOT VERIFIED |
+| Frontend App | "Functional" | ❌ NOT VERIFIED |
+| Database | None | ❌ REQUIRED FOR DEMO |
+
+**Demo Requirements (MANDATORY):**
 
-- `@uptime/solver` package with 20 passing tests
-- Demo presentation site (apps/presentation)
-- Research documentation
+1. Persistent database (not in-memory/hardcoded)
+2. Real data flow (API → DB → Frontend)
+3. Working solver integration
 
-**Demo URL:** http://localhost:4323/demo
+**Nice to Have:**
 
-**Next Steps:**
+- Excel export
+- Calendar view
 
-- Verify demo with client
-- Deploy to Vercel when ready
+**DO NOT DEMO until verified end-to-end.**
